test_cases.title: Editor successfully creates a task in their project
preconditions: A project exists with at least one member assigned the 'editor' role
The editor is authenticated and has an active session
steps: Authenticate as a user with the 'editor' role in the target project
Send a POST request (or use UI) to create a new task with valid required fields in the project
Observe the response or resulting state
test_cases.expected_result: The task is created successfully, a success response (e.g., HTTP 201) is returned, and the new task appears in the project's task list.

test_cases.title: Editor successfully edits any existing task in the project
preconditions: A project exists with at least two tasks, one created by a different user
The authenticated user has the 'editor' role in the project
steps: Send a PUT/PATCH request (or use UI) to update a task that was created by another user, changing at least one field (e.g., title or description)
Observe the response or resulting state
test_cases.expected_result: The task is updated successfully, a success response (e.g., HTTP 200) is returned, and the task reflects the new values.

test_cases.title: Editor successfully adds a comment to a task
preconditions: A project with at least one task exists
The authenticated user has the 'editor' role in the project
steps: Send a POST request (or use UI) to add a comment to an existing task with valid comment content
Observe the response or resulting state
test_cases.expected_result: The comment is created successfully, a success response (e.g., HTTP 201) is returned, and the comment is associated with the task.

test_cases.title: Editor is denied when attempting to delete the project
preconditions: The authenticated user has the 'editor' role in the project
steps: Send a DELETE request (or use UI) targeting the project itself
Observe the response or resulting state
test_cases.expected_result: The request is rejected with an authorization error (e.g., HTTP 403 Forbidden), and the project remains intact.

test_cases.title: Editor is denied when attempting to modify project-level settings
preconditions: A project exists with configurable project-level settings (e.g., name, visibility, member roles)
The authenticated user has the 'editor' role in the project
steps: Send a PUT/PATCH request (or use UI) to modify a project-level setting (e.g., change project name or visibility)
Observe the response or resulting state
test_cases.expected_result: The request is rejected with an authorization error (e.g., HTTP 403 Forbidden), and the project settings remain unchanged.

test_cases.title: Non-editor (viewer/unauthenticated user) is denied task creation
preconditions: The actor is either unauthenticated or assigned a role lower than 'editor' (e.g., 'viewer') in the project
steps: Authenticate as a user with a non-editor role (or send the request without authentication)
Send a POST request (or use UI) to create a new task in the project
Observe the response or resulting state
test_cases.expected_result: The request is rejected with an authorization or authentication error (e.g., HTTP 401 or HTTP 403), and no task is created.

assumptions: Role enforcement is applied server-side and cannot be bypassed by manipulating client-side state.
'Project-level settings' refers to attributes such as project name, description, visibility, and member role assignments, as the requirement does not enumerate them explicitly.
The system has at least one role with higher privileges than 'editor' (e.g., 'admin' or 'owner') that is permitted to delete projects and modify project settings.
A 'viewer' or equivalent lower-privilege role exists to support the negative permission test case.
Adding comments applies to task-level comments; no project-level commenting behavior is defined in this requirement.
notes: The requirement does not specify whether an editor can delete individual tasks (only project deletion is explicitly forbidden); this is a coverage gap and a test case for task deletion by an editor has been omitted pending clarification. The exact set of 'project-level settings' is not defined, which may require additional test cases once the settings catalogue is specified. The requirement does not address rate limiting, input validation on task/comment fields, or concurrent edit behavior.
